channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Czech police detain Russian priest over ‘white substance’ find
item.description: Moscow condemned the actions of the Czech police, calling the detainment a 'provocation'.

item.title: Israel to intensify Lebanon offensive to ‘crush’ Hezbollah
item.description: Prime Minister Netanyahu's order comes despite 'ceasefire' agreed with Lebanon last month, which was recently extended.

item.title: Bolivia’s president cuts salary in half amid protests
item.description: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz says he and his cabinet ministers will cut their salaries by 50 percent.

item.title: Trump pays tribute to US troops killed in war on Iran on Memorial Day
item.description: US President Donald Trump marked Memorial Day at Arlington National Cemetery, paying tribute to US troops killed.

item.title: Ex-Scottish National Party chief pleads guilty to embezzling funds
item.description: Peter Murrell admitted the offences at the High Court in Edinburgh after an investigation into the party's finances.

item.title: Trump dangles normalisation amid pro-Israel criticism of possible Iran deal
item.description: US president says he is 'mandatorily requesting' that regional countries establish formal relations with Israel.

item.title: THE KISWA: BEYOND THE CLOTH
item.description: Every Muslim on earth has faced it in prayer. Billions have seen it in photos. Almost nobody knows its story.

item.title: Kyiv’s Chornobyl Museum devastated in overnight Russian missile attack
item.description: Kyiv's Chornobyl Museum devastated in overnight Russian missile attack.

item.title: Father mourns daughter killed in Israeli strike on Gaza
item.description: A six-year-old Palestinian girl was killed by an Israeli strike on a displacement camp in Khan Younis.

item.title: Lebanon marks Liberation Day under Israeli bombardment
item.description: People in Lebanon have gathered to observe Liberation Day.

item.title: In Lebanon, everything and nothing has changed since 2000
item.description: A local war has gone regional, drawing in Iran and the US, but its root cause is unchanged: Palestine.

item.title: Bolivia’s embattled president says will take a 50 percent salary cut
item.description: Rodrigo Paz says the salary cut for him and cabinet ministers shows the government's 'commitment to the country'.

item.title: Mexico to host Iran team during 2026 FIFA World Cup amid US tensions
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum says United States did not want to host Iranian football team during tournament.

item.title: Israel escalates Gaza attacks as Netanyahu stalls ceasefire for polls
item.description: Analysts warn Netanyahu is exploiting a failing "ceasefire" to prolong the destruction of Gaza ahead of Israeli election

item.title: Subdued Lebanon Liberation Day celebrations under new Israeli occupation
item.description: Twenty-six years after Israeli forces were driven out of southern Lebanon, fears of a new occupation have emerged.

item.title: Tunisian court hands presidential critic Sonia Dahmani new jail term
item.description: Lawyer convicted for the second time this year as President Saied's crackdown on dissent intensifies.

item.title: Mexico steps in to host Iran’s World Cup team after US refusal
item.description: Mexico says it has agreed to host Iran’s World Cup team after the US declined to allow the squad to stay.

item.title: Over 1.5 million pilgrims begin Hajj amid regional tensions
item.description: The spiritual and physical challenge is described by pilgrims as a transformative and deeply meaningful journey.

item.title: How technology is shaping Hajj
item.description: With nearly two million worshippers performing Hajj this year, technology is key to ensuring everything runs smoothly.

item.title: Pope says AI must be ‘disarmed’ to prevent domination, exclusion, and death
item.description: In his first encyclical, Leo insists ownership of artificial intelligence data must not be left solely in private hands.

item.title: Rescuers search for survivors after hotel collapse in Philippines
item.description: Rescuers search for survivors after hotel collapse in Philippines.

item.title: Russia warns foreigners to leave Kyiv as it prepares ‘systematic strikes’
item.description: Moscow calls upcoming 'systematic strikes' on Ukrainian capital a response to Kyiv's recent attack in Luhansk.

item.title: Nearly 300 tourists rescued after cable cars halt mid-air
item.description: Around 300 people were stranded mid-air after the Gondola system suddenly halted in Indian-administered Kashmir.

item.title: Train bomb in Pakistan’s Baloch region: Why violence is on the rise
item.description: Deadly blast in Pakistan marks uptick in Balochistan separatist violence.

item.title: Pope Leo calls for ‘disarming’ of artificial intelligence
item.description: Pope Leo has warned that some AI-powered weapons systems are becoming ‘practically beyond human control'.
